title: Repo-Root Task Surface Historical Relabel And Demotion Execution
objective: Execute the Packet 096-selected slice by relabeling the current-looking parent-root bootstrap and Class A scaffold tasks in the active repo-root VS Code task surface and aligning the operator runbook so delegated execution stops treating those historical packet helpers as current defaults.
dependency_note: Packet 096 identified the repo-root VS Code task surface as the strongest remaining parent-root misrouting risk because it still exposed historical bootstrap and scaffold packets with current-looking labels. Packet 097 executes the smallest truthful repair by relabeling those tasks and matching the runbook wording.
execution_scope: Relabel the parent-root bootstrap and Class A scaffold task entries in `.vscode/tasks.json` so they read as historical surfaces inside the active repo workspace.
Update `docs/OPERATOR-BOOTSTRAP-RUNBOOK.md` so the task inventory and explanatory note match the new labels and routing posture.

constraints: Do not mutate runtime, host services, packages, or lockfiles.
Do not delete historical packet evidence by implication.
Do not reopen parent-root publication flow as a current operator default.

execution_rationale: The task list is a stronger delegated-execution surface than closed historical handoff prose, so relabeling the task entries directly reduces operator misrouting faster than deeper archival work.
Adding explicit `historical` markers preserves provenance value while making the current repo-root routing unambiguous.
This slice closes the selected discoverability defect without reopening runtime, hosting, or publication-boundary mechanics.
next_packet_candidate: Return the provenance-routing lane to hold unless another current-looking parent-root residue surface still misdirects active execution.
closure_note: Executed 2026-05-07 as the direct follow-on to Packet 096. Packet 097 relabels the strongest current-looking parent-root task residue in `.vscode/tasks.json` and aligns the operator runbook so historical packet helpers remain available for provenance review without reading like routine current staging actions.
